title: Gate-reminder hooks account for centralized Team-Lead gating
description: The builder Stop-hook gate-reminder and the scribe PostToolUse:Edit gate-reminder both assume the agent making an edit also runs the gate. Sprint 7 centralized gating on the Team Lead, so the Stop-hook fired repeatedly for Team-Lead-run gates and the PostToolUse:Edit reminder fired on each scribe CONTEXT.md/PROCESS.md edit mid-batch — accurate that a gate was due, but misattributing who runs it, producing harmless repeated noise. The reminder hooks should account for a gating model where the editor is not the gate-runner.
acceptance_criteria: The gate-reminder hooks distinguish a gate being due from who runs it, and do not imply the editing agent must run the gate
Under centralized Team-Lead gating the reminders do not produce repeated misattributed noise
A gate genuinely outstanding is still surfaced
notes: Filed from the Sprint 7 retro (obs 4), 2026-06-12. builder-owned (src hooks) when pulled.
